mappings.rationaleAI: Macromolecules covers the four main classes of biological molecules -- carbohydrates, lipids, proteins, and nucleic acids (DNA and RNA) -- directly mapping to organic chemicals participating in life processes.

Enzymes as biological catalysts with lock-and-key and induced fit models, reaction kinetics, cofactors, and regulatory mechanisms directly address the enzyme category of organic chemicals in life processes.

Hormones as chemical messengers covering insulin, adrenaline, oestrogen, testosterone, hypothalamus-pituitary feedback loops, and the history of endocrinology directly map to hormones of vertebrates and their general features.

The history of drugs covers narcotics, stimulants, anesthesia, morphine, cocaine, antibiotics, and the pharmaceutical industry, directly addressing drugs as chemicals that change physiological state, including drugs affecting the CNS and drug use/abuse.
Pheromones as chemical signals used by insects and mammals for sexual attraction, alarm signaling, and social organization directly map to hormonelike substances of invertebrates.

Immunisation covers vaccines that stimulate the immune response system and the scientific understanding of immunity, directly relevant to drugs affecting the immune response system and drugs derived from microorganisms.
Carbon's central role as the basis of all organic chemistry and life, forming long chains and complex molecules, underpins all organic chemicals participating naturally in life processes.
The history of disease covers antibiotics and germ theory, relevant to drugs derived from microorganisms and directed against disease organisms, as well as microbial toxins (biotoxins).
Infectious disease origins cover pathogens, antibiotic resistance, and evolutionary arms races between hosts and pathogens, relevant to chemotherapeutic drugs and microbial toxins.
Circadian rhythms governing hormone release and metabolism through molecular feedback loops relate directly to hormonal regulation and the relationship between endocrine and neural regulation.
Mitochondria's role in ATP synthesis through oxidative phosphorylation directly addresses ATP as the major carrier of chemical energy in living systems.
Vitalism's challenge by organic chemistry (Wohler's urea synthesis) and the eventual triumph of mechanistic biology provides the historical context for understanding chemicals in vital processes.
Parasitic strategies for immune evasion and host manipulation involve biotoxins and chemical interactions between organisms, relevant to biotoxins produced by microorganisms and animals.
